goal: Define the document-only Approval Readiness Contract after Human Gate Candidate without recording approval, enabling task creation, or calling Builder.
claims: BP-057 defines only a document-level Approval Readiness Contract after Human Gate Candidate.
BP-057 keeps approval effect, approval recording, task creation, execution, and live Builder calls blocked.
BP-057 does not implement UI, auth, secrets, DB, persistence, approval action, or Builder integration.
assumptions: Human Gate Candidate output is the required source for later Approval Readiness input.
Approval readiness can be assessed locally before any real approval action exists.
Effective approval needs later explicit auth, identity, persistence, audit, and task-create readiness decisions.
dod: The document defines required input, output, gates, blocked behavior, and non-goals.
The document explicitly prevents automatic approval, approval recording, Task Create, Execute, Approve, Push, Deploy, auth, secrets, DB, and live Builder calls.
stop_conditions: Runtime, fixture, package, or tool changes become necessary
Effective approval, auth, persistence, live Builder, DB, deploy, execution, task creation, approve, or push becomes necessary
prior_task_findings: BP-056 identified Approval Readiness Contract as the next safe boundary after the local Human Gate Candidate chain.
